## [06:00 - 09:00]

And so typically what we do is we'll publish the video on YouTube and then like Ashley said, across all the social channels. And then we'll come back in our ads. And we have several different campaigns that we run. Some are just strictly to buyers. Some are to our warm audiences. It kind of depends on the context of the video, but a lot of times like these product update videos specifically, we'll run those to our buyer audiences just to let them know like, hey, this new thing came out. Here's why it's cool. You should check it out. And we'll do that on Facebook, Instagram, YouTube, and Google. Depending on the context, it's pretty much just plastered across those platforms. And then if it's like educational, sometimes we'll actually even take that and roll it into our top line ads where we're just trying to get people into our system and our ecosphere where it's like, hey, this is kind of value like the educational piece that Ashley was showing with Ezra. We would actually probably run that top line in our ads on Facebook and Instagram, especially. And then eventually YouTube as well. But just to get people like pixeled so that we know that they watch that video, they've gotten added to our video view audiences or whatever audiences so then we can retarget them with our pre-sell articles or opt-in pages or webinars or whatever it is that we may be doing. We just kind of start there and then that trickles them down through the rest of the stuff that we have. So that's kind of like how we syndicate it and amplify it depending on the piece of content and what platform it's best for. One other note about that is occasionally when we use it for ads, we will make a shorter edit of the video if it's a longer one because ads kind of tend to do better if they're a little bit shorter and more punchy. So we'll kind of like edit down the core idea into like a maybe a 90-second video and then roll that out. Or we might roll them both.
